diff --git a/ipfs_datasets_py/logic/modal/compiler.py b/ipfs_datasets_py/logic/modal/compiler.py
index 03feae36..6d6ff74d 100644
--- a/ipfs_datasets_py/logic/modal/compiler.py
+++ b/ipfs_datasets_py/logic/modal/compiler.py
@@ -42,6 +42,7 @@ class ModalCompilerConfig:
     top_k_frames: int = 3
     frame_domain: Optional[str] = None
     frame_score_margin: float = 0.05
+    modal_family_count_margin: float = 0.15
 
 
 @dataclass(frozen=True)
@@ -142,6 +143,7 @@ class DeterministicModalCompiler:
         selected_frame = str(frame_candidates[0]["frame_id"]) if frame_candidates else None
         ambiguities = [
             *self._formula_ambiguities(modal_ir),
+            *self._family_ambiguities(encoding),
             *self._frame_ambiguities(frame_selections),
         ]
         if normalized_text and not modal_ir.formulas:
@@ -259,6 +261,39 @@ class DeterministicModalCompiler:
             )
         ]
 
+    def _family_ambiguities(
+        self,
+        encoding: SpaCyLegalEncoding,
+    ) -> List[ModalCompilationAmbiguity]:
+        family_counts = encoding.modal_family_counts()
+        if len(family_counts) < 2:
+            return []
+        ranked_families = sorted(
+            family_counts.items(),
+            key=lambda item: (-item[1], item[0]),
+        )
+        (top_family, top_count), (runner_up_family, runner_up_count) = ranked_families[:2]
+        total = sum(family_counts.values())
+        if total <= 0:
+            return []
+        margin = (float(top_count) - float(runner_up_count)) / float(total)
+        if margin > self.config.modal_family_count_margin:
+            return []
+        return [
+            ModalCompilationAmbiguity(
+                ambiguity_type="close_modal_family_counts",
+                message="Deterministic modal cue counts are too close to select one family confidently.",
+                candidate_ids=[top_family, runner_up_family],
+                metadata={
+                    "family_counts": dict(sorted(family_counts.items())),
+                    "margin": round(margin, 6),
+                    "runner_up_family_count": runner_up_count,
+                    "top_family_count": top_count,
+                    "total_modal_cues": total,
+                },
+            )
+        ]
+
 
 __all__ = [
     "DeterministicModalCompiler",
diff --git a/tests/unit_tests/logic/modal/test_modal_codec.py b/tests/unit_tests/logic/modal/test_modal_codec.py
index 4d47cd83..f614258d 100644
--- a/tests/unit_tests/logic/modal/test_modal_codec.py
+++ b/tests/unit_tests/logic/modal/test_modal_codec.py
@@ -143,6 +143,36 @@ def test_modal_compiler_decompiler_are_explainable_and_deterministic() -> None:
     }
 
 
+def test_modal_compiler_flags_close_modal_family_counts_as_ambiguity() -> None:
+    compiler = DeterministicModalCompiler(
+        ModalCompilerConfig(parser_backend="regex", frame_score_margin=0.0),
+        frame_selector=BM25FrameSelector(
+            (
+                FrameCandidate(
+                    frame_id="agency_jurisdiction",
+                    label="Agency jurisdiction",
+                    terms=("agency", "jurisdiction"),
+                ),
+            )
+        ),
+    )
+
+    compiled = compiler.compile(
+        "The agency has jurisdiction. The agency must issue guidance.",
+        document_id="family-ambiguity-doc",
+    )
+    by_type = {ambiguity.ambiguity_type: ambiguity for ambiguity in compiled.ambiguities}
+
+    assert "close_modal_family_counts" in by_type
+    assert by_type["close_modal_family_counts"].candidate_ids == ["deontic", "frame"]
+    assert by_type["close_modal_family_counts"].metadata["family_counts"] == {
+        "deontic": 1,
+        "frame": 1,
+    }
+    assert by_type["close_modal_family_counts"].metadata["margin"] == 0.0
+    assert "close_bm25_frame_scores" not in by_type
+
+
 def test_modal_decompiler_preserves_context_without_formula_style_text() -> None:
     codec = DeterministicModalLogicCodec(
         ModalLogicCodecConfig(parser_backend="regex", embedding_dimensions=8)
